command: bash -lc 'python3 -c "from pathlib import Path; import re, sys; base = Path(\".\"); files = {\"init\": base / \"src/guild_scroll/__init__.py\", \"pyproject\": base / \"pyproject.toml\", \"readme\": base / \"README.md\", \"test\": base / \"tests/test_cli.py\"}; versions = {\"init\": re.search(r\"__version__\\s*=\\s*\\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\", files[\"init\"].read_text()).group(1), \"pyproject\": re.search(r\"^version\\s*=\\s*\\\"([^\\\"]+)\\\"\", files[\"pyproject\"].read_text(), re.MULTILINE).group(1), \"readme\": re.search(r\"version-([0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+)\", files[\"readme\"].read_text()).group(1), \"test\": re.search(r\"([0-9]+\\.[0-9]+\\.[0-9]+)\", files[\"test\"].read_text()).group(1)}; sys.exit(0 if len(set(versions.values())) == 1 else 2)"'
description: Run this before a git commit to block the commit when version strings are out of sync across src/guild_scroll/__init__.py, pyproject.toml, the README.md version badge, and tests/test_cli.py.
